title: Weakness Ledger Optimisation Loop
goal: Run the weakness-ledger tracks in priority order, starting with high-priority broad-work decomposition and planning artifact integrity.
Use dynamic model-CLI evaluations to surface concrete agent ergonomics or workflow failures, then fix package, harness, docs, or prompt surfaces where the fix is bounded.
Continue until the ledger no longer contains major unaddressed weakness signals from the current evaluation loop, or route any remaining major weakness into explicit follow-up.
non_goals: Do not implement deferred GitHub issues #595, #627, or #661.
Do not redesign the model CLI harness architecture unless repeated evaluations prove the current shape cannot express the needed probe.
Do not turn adaptive dogfooding into a deterministic formal benchmark.

intent.outcome: The high-priority model weakness tracks are reprobed, concrete product/harness fixes are applied, and the ledger reflects no major unaddressed weakness from this loop.
intent.constraints: Run high-priority tracks first; keep #595/#627/#661 deferred; use qualitative judgement rather than mechanical scoring as the primary optimisation signal.
intent.latitude: Tune harness scenario prompts/scoring, compact workflow/doc surfaces, update ledger statuses, and create follow-up issues for real bugs or substantial product gaps.
intent.escalation: Escalate if the right fix requires broad workflow policy, external CI/review enforcement, generated CLI relocation, or unrelated backlog work.

execution.next_step: Archive this execplan and keep monitoring fixed tracks after future workflow, Memory, or harness changes.
execution.proof: Focused harness probes, focused tests, planning surface checks, and make check passed.

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: run final validation and close out if no new major weaknesses appear

intent_interpretation.literal request: Keep running the tracks in priority order and fixing surfaced issues until no major weaknesses remain.
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: Use the model weakness ledger as an adaptive optimisation loop, not a static report, and keep patching the surfaces that make agents misuse the package.
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Run high-priority broad-work and planning-artifact probes first, then medium-priority Memory probes if the high-priority tracks are no longer exposing major issues.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: scripts/model_cli_harness/**, tools/model-cli-harness/**, docs/**, README.md, package workflow/startup text if directly implicated, this execplan, and planning state.
execution_bounds.max changed files: About 20; stop and reassess before broad docs, generated output, or workflow architecture rewrites.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: focused model CLI harness probes for the touched tracks; uv run pytest tests/test_model_cli_harness.py -q; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; make check before closeout.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before changing generated CLI layout, external CI/review enforcement, or replacing the evaluation harness design.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Deferred issues #595/#627/#661, generated/python relocation, broad large-file remediation, or unrelated historical audit candidates.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs files outside the filled execution bounds.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, the promoted item, and the narrow files needed for the current implementation step.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Active loop order: broad-work decomposition, planning artifact integrity, then Memory routing if high-priority tracks are quiet.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: Run priority weakness-ledger tracks and fix surfaced issues until no major weakness remains.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: Keep #595/#627/#661 deferred; use package planning; do not mistake clean deterministic metrics for adequate qualitative evaluation.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: Which scenario evidence is significant, which bounded fixes to apply, whether to downgrade ledger priority/status, and which findings deserve issues.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: The correct fix requires broad host-repo policy, external enforcement, or work explicitly deferred by the user.

adaptive_assurance.reason: The lane changes agent-facing workflow/evaluation surfaces and may alter how future agents interpret broad work. It is not safety-critical, but regressions can degrade package dogfooding.
required_gates: major surfaced issues fixed or routed

decision_refs: Use qualitative judgement from model transcripts as the primary optimisation signal.

system_intent_alignment.slice shaping bias: Keep the slice bounded while carrying any larger follow-on through explicit continuation fields.
system_intent_alignment.broader-lane validation question: Did this slice advance the declared larger outcome, or only complete the local task?

completion_criteria: High-priority broad-work and planning-artifact-integrity tracks have fresh evaluation evidence.
Major surfaced failures are fixed directly or routed to explicit follow-up issues if too large for this lane.
The ledger statuses/priorities reflect current evidence rather than stale historical failures.
Planning state and repo checks pass.
The active execplan is closed out honestly.

closure_check.why this decision is honest: The current priority queue has no remaining major unaddressed weakness signals; remaining deferred issues #595, #627, and #661 stay deferred by user instruction.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: make check; planning surface check; focused model CLI harness reruns recorded in tools/model-cli-harness/model-task-weakness-ledger.json

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.
signals found: Copilot still created root README/PLANNING_STATE handoff files and unregistered execplans before prep-only guidance was tightened.
The prep-only scaffold was generic enough that the agent felt compelled to enrich or supplement it manually.
Copilot created a freehand workspace architecture doc and later deleted shipped templates because summary misclassified TEMPLATE.plan.json as active work.
Postmortem feedback passes spent tokens inspecting disposable fixtures and returned generic fixture advice even when supplied evidence was enough.
signals fixed: Shipped workflow payload and harness fixtures now say prep-only output is registered Planning state, not root handoff docs.
Planning help and new-plan --prep-only output now state allowed post-new-plan edits and forbid PLANNING_STATE.
Latest broad-work rerun created only canonical planning/state files and no product or root handoff files.
Planning help and WORKFLOW now keep broad handoff assumptions inside planning/decomposition records and distinguish future owner surfaces from existing next-action files.
Planning summary now excludes TEMPLATE.plan.json from active execplan projection, and the harness scores planning-only architecture/handoff side docs plus template deletion.
Memory probes show Copilot/Haiku uses the narrow testing-and-packaging Memory note before README edits and avoids duplicate durable capture.
Postmortem feedback prompt now says not to inspect the repo, run commands, or read files, and to answer only from supplied evidence.
signals routed: GitHub #733 created for the postmortem feedback token-use finding and implemented in this iteration.
improvement_signal_review.next owner: current execplan; next step is final validation and closeout

discard.summary: No Memory, docs, or config promotion was needed for local execution detail.
